solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int minAddToMakeValid(string s) {
        int left = 0, right = 0;
        for (char c : s) {
            if (c == '(') {
                left++;
            } else {
                if (left > 0) {
                    left--;
                } else {
                    right++;
                }
            }
        }
        return left + right;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a greedy approach to count the number of unmatched opening and closing parentheses. It iterates through the string once, keeping track of the balance of parentheses. If an unmatched closing parenthesis is encountered, it is counted as needing an opening parenthesis to balance it. At the end, the total number of unmatched parentheses (both opening and closing) gives the minimum number of insertions required.
